invariant: STEP 0.0 (R-MW-3, D28) — if current branch = master AND workstream branches present → HALT. A runner that skips STEP 0.0 proceeds silently past master state, violating the enforce-non-master guard.
owning_role: prompts/_orchestrator.md STEP 0.0 — Run git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD; if master/main: scan feature/* + bugfix/*; any present → HALT with branch list.
seed: Apply defect: STEP 0.0 block removed from orchestrator (orchestrator proceeds directly to STEP 0.1 without branch check)
Run against scenario-master-halt state (current branch = master, feature/my-feature + bugfix/fix-rate present)
Expected: orchestrator proceeds past STEP 0.0 without HALT (no branch-guard message)
load_bearing_assertion: Golden orchestrator: HALT with 'On master with in-flight workstream branches: ...' emitted. Defect orchestrator: no HALT — proceeds to STEP 0.1 / frontier-scan on master. Value-parity fails: step_0_0_halt == false vs golden true.
expected_verdict: value-parity FAIL — no-enforce-non-master: orchestrator proceeds on master without HALT
separates_from_golden_by: HALT message presence + step_0_0_halt flag. Golden: HALT fires with branch list. Defect: no HALT, orchestrator runs on master — any frontier-scan output = discrimination signal.
